page: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs, which correspond to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or a GPU, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier Geekbench multi-core score of 20,959.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 83,206.18. Single-core performance is also highly competitive, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2,200.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 5,116.7 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 196.96 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though large-block write bandwidth is more moderate at 82.79 GB/sec. The hardware excels in specialized tasks, demonstrating top-tier performance in software compilation, static web serving at 104.85 GB/sec, and Redis SET operations at 113.83 million operations per second. It also exhibits strong capabilities in LLM inference, processing 303.91 tokens per second for a medium 7B model prompt.

The c8i-flex.16xlarge offers a highly cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU acceleration. Its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it highly optimized for raw processing power rather than memory-heavy datasets. The absence of bundled local storage means users must rely on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider. This server is best suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, high-throughput web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 7 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, software compilation, and static web serving, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and lack of local storage represent clear tradeoffs, its high-speed memory bandwidth and strong CPU benchmarks make it highly cost-efficient for raw compute tasks. It is best suited for software development pipelines, high-throughput web servers, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i-flex.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier performance for compilation and web serving.

tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized server featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
